campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary zoning rule and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth scholarships
Extension of temporary zoning ordinance #2326
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge project over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application submission for bridge planning and feasibility study

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on a new rule or policy, then decided to move forward with it quickly by skipping the standard first reading process. The ordinance will be voted on at the next council meeting instead of going through the usual multiple-reading procedure.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input on a new ordinance
First reading of the ordinance was waived to speed up the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at the next regular council meeting
Specific topic of the ordinance not detailed in the agenda provided

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to approve several administrative items and policies. Key decisions included finalizing the city's weed control program, establishing new rules for how the council operates, updating affordable housing transfer policies, and approving two flag raisings to recognize Pride Month and Juneteenth. The council also heard a transportation presentation and discussed plans for implementing the city's housing goals.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion for 2026
New City Council operating procedures and norms
Affordable housing unit transfer policies
Flag raisings for Pride Month and Juneteenth
VTA transportation authority presentation and housing implementation schedule

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several construction and service contracts during this April 2026 meeting. The main decisions included hiring QLM, Inc. for a construction project, bringing in Dudek for professional services, and joining a statewide insurance program called CJPIA to help manage the city's insurance costs.
keyTopics: Construction contract approved with QLM, Inc.
Professional services agreement with Dudek
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority for better insurance rates
Multiple contract amendments and agreements updated
Art transfer agreement discussion

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review programs and services for young people in the city, including updates on youth empowerment initiatives and summer safety programs. They also heard reports on code enforcement activities and plans to improve library facilities and services.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and summer safety grant programs
Children and Youth Services Master Plan progress
Youth Intervention Services annual performance
Code Enforcement Department annual report
Library facility improvements and customer experience upgrades

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included both in-person and online participation. Residents were able to join the meeting virtually via Zoom or phone to listen and provide public comments on agenda items.

Public comment procedures for remote participants
Specific agenda items not detailed in the provided meeting notice

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held their regular meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official minutes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission regular meeting
Personal stories shared by Commissioners 5, 6, and 7
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community programs including a food and budget initiative called Budget and Bites, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and summer job opportunities for young people. The council also approved minutes from their previous March meeting and opened the floor for residents to speak about any city issues for up to three minutes each.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth organization
City of Cupertino Summer Jobs program
Approval of March 11, 2026 meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person and remote options so residents could participate however works best for them. The council provided multiple ways for the public to join and submit comments on agenda items.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format - attend in person or via Zoom
Remote participation options for residents who can't attend physically
Written public comment submission process available online

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting to approve several administrative and financial matters, including joining a tax board partnership with the county, considering fee waivers for a local educational organization, and awarding a construction contract for an upcoming project. The council also discussed updating the city's housing and community development plan and reviewed new requests from individual council members.
keyTopics: Joining Franchise Tax Board partnership with county
Fee waiver and donation request from Milpitas Community Educational
Construction contract award and project approval
Update to 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New workplan items requested by council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed and approved the financial plan for Palo Alto's gas utility for the next fiscal year, which included adjusting gas rates for residential and commercial customers. The meeting also involved approving previous meeting minutes and discussing how money would be moved between different city accounts to support the utility's operations.
keyTopics: Gas rate changes for homes and businesses
Fiscal Year 2027 gas utility budget and financial forecast
Transfer of money to the city's general fund
Previous meeting minutes approval
